children.text: Designation of posts; time of designation and implementation; determination of competency standards
In order to carry out the purposes of

children.tail: and to help ascertain the relationship between foreign language competence and the effectiveness of representation of the United States abroad, the Secretary of State shall designate as model foreign language competence posts at least two Foreign Service posts in countries where English is not the common language. Such designation shall be made no later than
, and shall be implemented so that no later than

, each Government employee permanently assigned to those posts shall possess an appropriate level of competence in the language common to the country where the post is located. The Secretary of State shall determine appropriate levels of language competence for employees assigned to those posts by reference to the nature of their functions and the standards employed by the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center.

children.text: Continuation; report to Congress concerning operation of posts and advantages of meeting competency requirements
The posts designated under subsection (a) shall continue as model foreign language competence posts at least until

children.tail: . The Secretary of State shall submit no later than

, a report to the Speaker of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate describing the operation of such posts and the costs, advantages and disadvantages associated with meeting the foreign language competence requirements of this section.

children.text: The Secretary of State may authorize exceptions to the requirements of this section if he determines that unanticipated exigencies so require.

children.tail: substituted “George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center” for “Foreign Service Institute”.

, (25), amended subsec. (c) identically, striking out at end “Such exceptions shall be annually reported to the Speaker of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate.”

children.text: Expansion of Model Foreign Language Competence Posts
In order to carry out the purposes of section 702 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 [

children.tail: ], and in light of the positive report issued on
, by the Department of State, as required by section 2207 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 [

], the Secretary of State shall designate as model foreign language competence posts a minimum of six Foreign Service posts, representing the Department of State’s five geographic bureaus, in countries where English is not the common language. Such designation shall be made not later than 120 days after the date of enactment of this Act [
], and shall be implemented so that not later than
, in the case of non-hard language posts, and

, in the case of hard language posts, each Government employee permanently assigned to those posts shall possess an appropriate level of competence in the language common to the country where the post is located. The Secretary of State shall determine appropriate levels of language competence for employees assigned to those posts by reference to the nature of their functions and the standards employed by the George P. Shultz National Foreign Affairs Training Center.

children.text: ‘Hard Language Country’ Post To Be Designated
At least one of the posts designated under subsection (a) shall be in a ‘hard language’ country, as identified in the report to the Under Secretary of State for Management of

children.tail: , entitled ‘Hard Language Proficiency in the Foreign Service’. Such post shall be in one of the countries where the official or principal language is Arabic, Chinese, Japanese, or Russian.

children.text: The posts designated under subsection (a) shall continue as model foreign language posts at least until

children.tail: , in the case of non-hard language posts, and
, in the case of hard language posts.

children.text: The Secretary of State may authorize exceptions to the requirements of this section if—
he determines that unanticipated exigencies so require; and
he immediately reports such exceptions to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.

The posts designated under subsection (a) may not include Dakar, Senegal, or Montevideo, Uruguay. The report required under subsection (c) shall include progress made in these posts in maintaining the high foreign language standards achieved under the initial pilot program.
There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.”
